item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really, really want to upvote this, but the journal's progression and the general narrative of like, "stoner fifthists" is just too similar to -1958 for me to in good conscience upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In his defense, he was really, really baked when he wrote that entry.

As for the rest? Sometimes he's sober, sometimes not. 3605 will mess you up regardless of what substances you consume, but combining it with drugs has…interesting results.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am irrationally angry with this dead fictional cultist for the phrase 'Doctor Who's telephone box'.

item.title: Notes
item.content:encoded:
The expungement here is designed specifically to address many criticisms people have about it in general, specifically that it's lazy or a cop-out. Much like 2317, I tried to use redaction as a way to create buildup for the final connection between 3255 and 3605. If I wanted to, I could fill in the redactions for all but one of these (which that is I will leave for the reader to figure out).

So in a way it's not truly redacting, at least not out-of-universe, since most of what was hidden is revealed at the end. Hope it worked well.

I'm otherwise fully satisfied with how this turned out, excepting one element: the order of the documentation. I debated a lot whether or not I should put the data or the journals first, and ultimately decided on the latter because I think it works better as a lead-in to the strangeness of the photos. At the same time, the final few logs seem more like a conclusory note for an SCP than the end of a section.

Dunno. Evidently enough people like it the way I wrote it for it to stick around, so I guess I'll leave 3605 as is for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This makes me think of the Blue cult from Earthbound, in a good way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quite enjoyable! It fitted well with my Fifthist headcanon while also showing how exposure to Fifthist anomalies is not good for your physical or mental health.

But the comet is so *beautiful*…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When Tanhony dropped his Fifthist skip, it occurred to me that I had never tackled the group myself despite all of my SCPs ending in -5. Let's fix that, shall we?

Needless to say, this article was directly inspired by and owes its existence to SCP-3255.

Image Sources: 1, 2. Both are in the public domain in the USA; the author of the first died in 1956, and the second in 1963.
